callout.text: No identity. No time. No tier.
WU credits land after final approval
Empty queue means work is flowing

narration.text: This is the Review page. Empty today — and that's worth pausing on. Reviewers find their queue here in the sidebar, the same shell every role uses, just a different view. This account is a coordinator, so what you see is the coordinator's queue. A reviewer signs in, and the same slot fills with submissions awaiting their judgment. When that happens, those gray cards in the main area become real submissions — one per piece of work waiting to be judged. And here's the rule that makes review fair: blind. The reviewer never sees who did the work, how long it took, or whether they're Senior, Normal, or Intern. Just the work. Work Unit credits aren't awarded until the highest-tier reviewer approves. One signal, one moment, no partial credit for almost-done. So an empty queue isn't a bug — it's a green light. Nothing is stuck waiting on a reviewer right now. Honest about who's looking, blind to who did it, fair on what gets credit. That's review in WorkBlock.
